_README: Raw placement FACTS for the GAA All-Ireland Senior Football Championship, 1887-2025 (1888 omitted: championship unfinished). Contains NO points - points are derived by build.py from params.json. Each year lists the provincial finals (winner/beaten finalist, plus beaten provincial semi-finalists in semi_final_losers for the no-quarter-final knockout eras 1928-2000 and 2020-21, derived from the match archive) and the All-Ireland series finishing stages, plus all_ireland.final detail (scoreline, venue, attendance, replay, captain, manager). GENERATED by assemble.py from the audited files in db/sources/ - edit those, not this file, then re-run assemble.py. 'best finish only' is applied at scoring time. Stage lists: semi_final_losers, quarter_final_losers (early-era extra rounds + 2023+ preliminary QFs are prelim_qf_losers), super8_group_exits (2018-19), group_exits (2023+ round-robin). For 1929-1989 the two semi_final_losers are derived as the provincial champions who did not reach the final.

final.notes: First All-Ireland final; played 29 April 1888.
Replay abandoned amid crowd trouble; Dublin awarded the title.
London contested the final as the 'away' champion.
Kerry refused to travel in a rail dispute; Louth awarded the title by walkover.
First final at the newly named Croke Park.
Wexford's fourth title in a row.
Title awarded after disqualifications; Galway named champions, Cavan special runners-up. The 3-02 v 1-02 line refers to the related Central Council final.
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
First final for the Sam Maguire Cup.
Kerry's four-in-a-row begins to take shape (third of four).
Kerry complete four-in-a-row (1929-32).
First Ulster team to win the Sam Maguire.
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
The only All-Ireland final played outside Ireland.
Mayo back-to-back champions (1950-51).
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
Derry's first final.
Sam Maguire crosses the border for the first time.
Record All-Ireland final attendance.
Galway three-in-a-row begins (1964-66).
Galway complete three-in-a-row.
First final played over 80 minutes.
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
Start of the Heffernan-era Dublin revival.
Kerry four-in-a-row (1978-81).
Offaly deny Kerry a historic five-in-a-row with a late Seamus Darby goal.
The 'twelve apostles' final; Dublin won with 12 men.
GAA centenary final.
Tyrone's first final.
Won after a replay (first game drawn 1-09 each).
Donegal's first All-Ireland.
Derry's first All-Ireland.
Won after a replay (first game drawn 0-12 each).
Won after a replay (first game drawn).
First champions via the qualifier (back-door) route.
Armagh's first All-Ireland.
Tyrone's first All-Ireland; first all-Ulster final.
Dublin's first title since 1995.
Won after a replay (first game drawn 2-09 to 0-15).
Dublin three-in-a-row.
Won after a replay; Dublin's historic five-in-a-row (first game drawn 1-16 each).
Played behind closed doors (COVID-19); December final. Dublin six-in-a-row.
Played at 50% capacity (COVID-19).
Armagh's second All-Ireland.
First final under the 2025 rule changes (two-pointers); Kerry's 39th title.
